ddls:
CREATE TABLE "calendars" (
  "calendar_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each calendar */,
  "calendar_name" TEXT /* Name of the calendar */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the calendar */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the department the calendar belongs to */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the location the calendar is associated with */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the calendar was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the calendar was last updated */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the calendar is active or not */,
  "calendar_type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the type of calendar (e.g., public, private) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("calendar_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_calendars_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_calendars_calendar_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("calendar_type_id") REFERENCES calendar_types ("calendar_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_calendars_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the department */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the department */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the manager of the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_departments_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("manager_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the location */,
  "capacity" INTEGER /* Maximum capacity of the location */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the location */,
  "location_type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the type of location (e.g., conference room, meeting room) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_locations_location_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_type_id") REFERENCES location_types ("location_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "event_types" (
  "event_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each event type */,
  "event_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the event type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the event type */,
  "event_category_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the category of the event (e.g., meeting, training) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_types_event_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_category_id") REFERENCES event_categories ("event_category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "events" (
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each event */,
  "calendar_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the calendar where the event is scheduled */,
  "event_name" TEXT /* Name of the event */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the event */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the event */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the event */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the location where the event is held */,
  "event_type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the type of event */,
  "created_by" TEXT /* User who created the event */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the event was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the event was last updated */,
  "event_status_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the status of the event (e.g., scheduled, cancelled) */,
  "parent_event_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the parent event (for hierarchical events) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_events_event_status_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_status_id") REFERENCES event_statuses ("event_status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_events_event_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_type_id") REFERENCES event_types ("event_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_events_calendar_id FOREIGN KEY ("calendar_id") REFERENCES calendars ("calendar_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_events_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "event_participants" (
  "participant_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each participant */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* ID of the event the participant is attending */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user participating in the event */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the participant (e.g., confirmed, pending, cancelled) */,
  "registered_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the participant registered for the event */,
  "attendance_status_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the attendance status of the participant (e.g., attended, absent) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("participant_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_participants_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES events ("event_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_participants_attendance_status_id FOREIGN KEY ("attendance_status_id") REFERENCES attendance_statuses ("attendance_status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_participants_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the department the user belongs to */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, manager, employee) */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "event_resources" (
  "resource_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each resource */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* ID of the event the resource is required for */,
  "resource_name" TEXT /* Name of the resource */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the resource required */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the resource (e.g., available, allocated, unavailable) */,
  "resource_type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the type of resource (e.g., equipment, material) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("resource_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_resources_resource_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("resource_type_id") REFERENCES resource_types ("resource_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_resources_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES events ("event_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "event_conflicts" (
  "conflict_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each conflict */,
  "event_id_1" INTEGER /* ID of the first event involved in the conflict */,
  "event_id_2" INTEGER /* ID of the second event involved in the conflict */,
  "conflict_type" TEXT /* Type of conflict (e.g., time, resource) */,
  "resolution_status" TEXT /* Status of the conflict resolution (e.g., pending, resolved) */,
  "resolution_notes" TEXT /* Notes about the conflict resolution */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("conflict_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "event_reminders" (
  "reminder_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each reminder */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* ID of the event the reminder is set for */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the reminder */,
  "reminder_time" TEXT /* Time when the reminder should be sent */,
  "sent_status" TEXT /* Status of the reminder (e.g., pending, sent) */,
  "reminder_type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the type of reminder (e.g., email, SMS) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reminder_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_reminders_reminder_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("reminder_type_id") REFERENCES reminder_types ("reminder_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_reminders_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES events ("event_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_reminders_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "calendar_types" (
  "calendar_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each calendar type */,
  "calendar_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the calendar type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the calendar type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("calendar_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "location_types" (
  "location_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location type */,
  "location_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the location type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the location type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "event_categories" (
  "event_category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each event category */,
  "event_category_name" TEXT /* Name of the event category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the event category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "event_statuses" (
  "event_status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each event status */,
  "event_status_name" TEXT /* Name of the event status */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the event status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "attendance_statuses" (
  "attendance_status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each attendance status */,
  "attendance_status_name" TEXT /* Name of the attendance status */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the attendance status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("attendance_status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "resource_types" (
  "resource_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each resource type */,
  "resource_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the resource type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the resource type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("resource_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reminder_types" (
  "reminder_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each reminder type */,
  "reminder_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the reminder type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the reminder type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reminder_type_id")
)
